steps.title: Identify the researchers' measure of relaxation
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that “a reduced heart rate is considered a measure of relaxation.” The researchers measured heart rates to test whether lullabies relax infants, so supporting evidence must demonstrate that the lullaby produced lower heart rates.

steps.title: Apply the measure to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice C directly demonstrates the effect the researchers measured—substantially lower infants' heart rates during the lullaby than during the non-lullaby. The remaining choices either measure a different parameter (irregularity rather than reduction), rely on different evidence (parental preference), or include irrelevant context. That matches choice C — C is correct.
